We investigated the role of EGFR in Parkinson disease using a cohort of 550 patients. Methods included qPCR with statistical significance assessed at p<0.05. Results indicated significant upregulation in disease tissue. These findings suggest the need for further mechanistic study.

Background: Parkinson disease remains a leading cause of morbidity. Methods: We performed Western blot on 1454 samples. Results: Expression of VEGF correlated with disease severity (r=0.69). Conclusion: VEGF is a candidate biomarker.
